seed_instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting.
org_constraint: Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding jargon and ensuring clarity.
new_constraint: Write the email in a humorous and witty tone.
instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting. Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding jargon and ensuring clarity. Write the email in a humorous and witty tone.
response:
Subject: My Resignation (Because I'm officially running out of coffee beans)

Hi [Boss's Name],

I hope you're having a Tuesday that isn't quite as chaotic as the rest of the week.

As we probably discussed earlier this morning, I've decided to put in my two weeks' notice. My last day will be [Your Last Day].

Before I go, I need to let you know that leaving is less about me needing a sabbatical to stare at trees and more about my brain finally deciding to upgrade its operating system. Currently, everything runs slightly slower than it used to, but after three years here, I can honestly say I've reached maximum capacity for "daily stand-ups" and "urgent" text messages.

Please consider me relieved to be trading in my hoodie and sneakers for something that doesn't require an explanation regarding why I left my office clothes in the cubicle from 2019.

While I've learned a lot while trying to figure out how to manage [mention one specific project or team member], I promise to remain fiercely committed until my final hour. As for what comes next? Well, that remains strictly off-limits. If I were to tell you where I'm going, the question would simply become "Where are *you* now?" and I'd have to answer "Somewhere else." But if you really want to know, think of it as a corporate sabbatical where the location changes every six months based on my mood.

Thanks for believing in me during the tough times when I thought I had forgotten how to breathe. I wish you nothing but success, and I hope we can maintain our cordial connection without any awkward silence.

Cheers,

[Your Name]<|im_end|>
